target_emits.name: create_server_group
target_emits.purpose: Create the server-group in the Library. Members (auth_server[]) map to an ordered servers[] array of {server-name, position}; the referenced auth-servers must already exist (central:auth_server runs first).
target_emits.iteration_notes: One POST per source server_group_prof record.

target_emits.name: assign_server_group
target_emits.purpose: Assign the server-group to the target scope so the Mobility Gateway aaa-profile can reference it. Multi-device-function array packed into a single POST.

config-assignment: {one entry per device_function in target_meta.device_functions: { scope-id, device-function, profile-type='server-groups', profile-instance='{name}' }}

target_emits.iteration_notes: One POST; all device-functions share the array.

target_meta.device_function_filtering_rule: Server-groups are referenced by the gateway aaa-profile; the AOS 8 -> Central migration targets MOBILITY_GW. Operators with AP-direct (bridge) auth can override device_functions in runtime_values.

target_scope_id_resolution.rule: Central scope_id is supplied by the consuming skill at runtime, derived from the AOS 8 server_group_prof record's binding scope.
target_scope_id_resolution.input: Source binding scope (string path / identifier); skill-specific

dependencies.depends_on: central:auth_server — server-group members reference auth-servers by name (servers[].server-name). Each AOS 8 server_group_prof.auth_server[] member names an auth-server; the referenced auth-server must exist in Central before this server-group POST. Run central:auth_server FIRST.

depended_by.relation: the gateway aaa-profile references server-groups by name
depended_by.notes: AOS 8 aaa_prof.dot1x_server_group / mba_server_group / rad_acct_sg name a server_group_prof; the Central aaa-profile references the server-group by name. Run server-group BEFORE the aaa-profile translation.

objects.notes: REST schema 'server_group_prof' (CLI noun: aaa server-group). 6 fields per the vendored AOS 8 OAS: sg_name (name), auth_server[] (ordered member list — each member: name + optional fqdn/all/operator), derivation_rules_vlan_role[] (server-derivation rules), fail_thru, load_balance, server_group_prof_clone. Live-verified across 9 groups in the maintainer's tenant (all single/multi RADIUS members).

name.to: Central server-group 'name' (path param step 1, profile-instance step 2, body 'name')

servers.notes: Maps the ordered AOS 8 auth_server[] member list to Central servers[] preserving order (1-based position = auth precedence). Members without a name dropped. Member _flags.inherited is NOT a drop signal (it just reflects the group being authored at an ancestor scope); record-level inheritance filtering is the consumer's job. Referenced auth-servers must pre-exist (dependency on central:auth_server).

unmapped_fields.from: derivation_rules_vlan_role[] (server-group server-derivation rules: attribute / operand / operator / prio)
unmapped_fields.reason: AOS 8 server-group VLAN/role derivation rules map to Central's server-group 'assignment-rules' (ArubaDynamicAssignment_AssignmentRulesConfig) — a richer structure (sequence-id / attribute / operator / assign-action) needing its own per-rule mapping. Deferred to a v2 once a configured sample is available; surface as OPERATOR-MAP so the gap is visible.

unmapped_fields.from: fail_thru / load_balance (server-group fail-through + load-balancing flags), server_group_prof_clone
unmapped_fields.reason: fail_thru/load_balance have candidate Central counterparts but field names are unverified; clone is a CLI authoring convenience, not a profile attribute. Map per-field once verified.

ignored_variants.reason: AOS 8 ships built-in server-groups. Consumer filters _flags.default / _flags.system / _flags.inherited (at definition scope) BEFORE calling emit_calls, matching the central:auth_server / net_group / role convention.

draft_notes: v1 translation, Gateway-targeted. POST /server-groups/{name} then config-assignment to MOBILITY_GW. Simple Paradigm-B: per-field key_mappings (name + servers), no preprocessing — the servers[] array map is a single 1-arg transform (aos8_server_group_members).

TYPE DEFAULT: body emits a literal type='RADIUS' — the dominant case for AOS 8 WLAN auth server-groups (live-verified: all 9 groups on the maintainer's tenant are RADIUS). TACACS / mixed-member groups need the type overridden; a v2 can derive it from the member auth-servers' types (cross-object, via runtime_values) the way central:auth_server correlates CoA. Surface as a caveat to the operator.

DEPENDENCY: members reference auth-servers by name (servers[].server-name). Run central:auth_server BEFORE this. In turn the gateway aaa-profile references server-groups by name (dot1xauth-server-group / macauth-server-group) — run server-group before the aaa-profile translation.
ORDERING: AOS 8 auth_server[] order is auth precedence; preserved as 1-based servers[].position. Inherited members filtered.

NEEDS-PREVIEW: config-assignment profile-type ('server-groups') and whether a server-group needs its own scope assignment (vs being referenced indirectly via the aaa-profile) are preview-confirm. assignment-rules (derivation) deferred to v2.
Idempotency: SHARED POST for an existing server-group name returns HTTP 409; consumer handles 409 idempotently.
